machinery_genes.role: AAA+ ATPase that recycles the contracted sheath; essential apparatus component of T6SS-3
machinery_genes.quote: The secretion of YPK_0952 was dramatically diminished with deletion of clpV3, but not with deletion of clpV1, clpV2, or clpV4, further indicating that YPK_0952 is a T6SS effector mainly associated with T6SS-3
machinery_genes.note: ClpV/TssH ATPase of the YPIII T6SS-3 cluster. Song et al 2020 (Nat Commun) name clpV3 as the T6SS-3 ATPase whose deletion abolishes secretion of the T6SS-3 effector YPK_0952. The companion regulation paper (Gueguen et al 2013, PLoS One) independently deletes clpV [YpsIP31758_1361] in reference strain IP31758 as 'encoding an essential component of the secretion apparatus' of T6SS-3 (cluster YpsIP31758_1354-_1379).

machinery_genes.role: large inner-membrane component (IcmF) of the trans-envelope membrane complex; interacts with TssL and TssJ
machinery_genes.quote: we show that the tssM gene associated with the Yersinia pseudotuberculosis T6SS-3 gene cluster is also subjected to transcriptional frameshifting
machinery_genes.note: TssM3 = icmF of the Y. pseudotuberculosis T6SS-3 cluster; Gueguen et al 2014 give locus YpsIP31758_1373 (icmF/tssM3) in reference strain IP31758 and show it encodes the ~130-kDa TssM membrane-complex protein. Strain transfer: apparatus characterized in IP31758, same T6SS-3 family system as in YPIII (NC_010465.1).

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane component of the trans-envelope membrane complex (anchors the phage-like structure to the cell envelope)
machinery_genes.quote: This membrane complex is composed of the TssL and TssM inner membrane proteins and the TssJ outer membrane lipoprotein
machinery_genes.note: Named as the conserved T6SS membrane-complex inner-membrane partner of TssM in the same paper that characterizes the Y. pseudotuberculosis T6SS-3 tssM3 gene. Component-level (general T6SS) naming rather than an IP31758/YPIII locus-specific functional study; included because TssM3 is documented for this cluster and TssL is its obligate membrane-complex partner. Medium confidence.

machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane lipoprotein of the trans-envelope membrane complex (binds the TssM C-terminal periplasmic domain)
machinery_genes.quote: The interaction with TssJ has been characterized and involves contacts between a specific loop of the lipoprotein and the 150 last residues of TssM. This interaction is critical for T6SS function as disruption of the TssM-TssJ interaction abolishes Hcp release in the culture supernatant.
machinery_genes.note: Named as the conserved OM lipoprotein of the membrane complex in the paper characterizing the Y. pseudotuberculosis T6SS-3 tssM3 gene; the TssM3 C-terminal domain whose frameshift production is studied is precisely the region that binds TssJ. Component-level naming, not an IP31758/YPIII locus-specific study. Medium confidence.

machinery_genes.role: hexameric inner tube (secreted); Hcp release in the supernatant is the functional readout of T6SS-3 assembly
machinery_genes.quote: the Hcp protein forms hexameric rings that stack on each other to assemble a tube resembling the internal tube of phages and tipped by a trimer of VgrG
machinery_genes.note: Hcp is machinery (secreted tube) per the apparatus brief. The same paper uses 'Hcp1 release' as the assay for Y. pseudotuberculosis-related T6SS-3 function and shows the T6SS-3 cluster is functional. No YPIII/IP31758-specific hcp3 locus is individually named in the literature found; quote is the general T6SS tube definition from the T6SS-3 tssM3 paper. Lower confidence on the exact T6SS-3 hcp locus.

machinery_genes.role: trimeric spike at the tip of the Hcp tube (secreted; cell-puncturing device)
machinery_genes.quote: the Hcp protein forms hexameric rings that stack on each other to assemble a tube resembling the internal tube of phages and tipped by a trimer of VgrG, which shares a fold similar to the trimeric bacteriophage gp27-gp5 hub
machinery_genes.note: VgrG is machinery (secreted spike) per the apparatus brief. General T6SS spike definition from the paper that characterizes the Y. pseudotuberculosis T6SS-3 tssM3 gene; Gueguen et al 2013 also note an isolated vgrG gene (YpsIP31758_0696) outside the clusters, so vgrG copies exist but the specific T6SS-3 vgrG locus is not individually characterized in the literature found. Lower confidence on the exact T6SS-3 vgrG locus.

machinery_genes.role: contractile sheath subunits (wrap the Hcp tube; contract to fire the spike)
machinery_genes.quote: This internal tube is wrapped into a structure composed of the TssB and TssC subunits
machinery_genes.note: Conserved T6SS sheath (TssB/VipA + TssC/VipB) named in the paper characterizing the Y. pseudotuberculosis T6SS-3 tssM3 gene. Component-level naming; specific T6SS-3 tssB3/tssC3 loci not individually characterized in the literature found. Listed as a single entry because the two genes are named together as the sheath. Medium-low confidence.

machinery_genes.role: spike-sharpening tip protein that caps the VgrG spike
machinery_genes.quote: we searched the Yersinia pseudotuberculosis YPIII genome for genes containing the Proline-Alanine-Alanine-aRginine (PAAR) domain, a conserved effector-targeting domain that is linked or adjacent to numerous known T6SS effectors. A gene locus encoding multiple hypothetical T6SS effector-immunity pairs was identified (YPK_0952-0958)... Both the first and last open-reading frame (ORF) of this locus contain PAAR domains.
machinery_genes.note: PAAR is machinery (spike tip) per the apparatus brief. Song et al 2020 identify PAAR-domain ORFs flanking the T6SS-3 effector locus in YPIII (YPK_0952-0958). YPK_0952 itself carries an N-terminal PAAR domain fused to a C-terminal pyocin toxin domain, so it is an evolved/cargo PAAR effector (counted as an effector, not listed here as pure machinery); the last ORF of the locus (YPK_0958) is a PAAR-only spike component. PAAR listed as machinery family; exact pure-PAAR locus = YPK_0958 (note only).

notes: System: T6SS-3 is the third of (at least) four/five complete Type VI secretion gene clusters in Yersinia pseudotuberculosis. In the YPIII genome (NC_010465.1, YPK_ locus tags) T6SS-3 delivers the antibacterial nuclease effectors YPK_0952 (PAAR-pyocin DNase), YPK_0954/Tce1 (DNase) and TseR (Ntox44 RNase); it mediates contact-dependent and contact-independent interbacterial killing and gut colonization. STRAIN TRANSFER: most apparatus-gene naming comes from the reference strain IP31758 (YpsIP31758_ locus tags), where Gueguen et al place the complete T6SS-3 cluster at YpsIP31758_1354-_1379 (two divergent operons: _1361-_1354 and _1362-_1379). YPIII and IP31758 carry the same T6SS-3 family system; Song et al 2020 study T6SS-3 directly in YPIII via the clpV3 deletion, so the apparatus transfers. WHAT IS DOCUMENTED GENE-BY-GENE FOR THIS CLUSTER: ClpV3/TssH ATPase (clpV3 in YPIII; YpsIP31758_1361 in IP31758, named 'essential component of the secretion apparatus'); TssM3/IcmF inner-membrane component (YpsIP31758_1373); and PAAR spike-tip ORFs flanking the YPK_0952-0958 effector locus. Hcp (tube), VgrG (spike) and TssB-TssC (sheath), plus TssL and TssJ (membrane complex), are named as the conserved Tss components in the same papers that characterize this cluster but are NOT individually mapped to T6SS-3 loci in the literature found, hence PARTIAL and lower/medium confidence on those rows. NOT FOUND (gaps): explicit T6SS-3-specific loci/quotes for TssA, TssE, TssF, TssG, TssK; these almost certainly exist in the _1354-_1379 cluster but no paper names them individually, so they are deliberately omitted (no naming quote = not listed). EXCLUDED as non-machinery: delivered toxin effectors and immunities (YPK_0952 PAAR-pyocin, Tce1/Tci1 = YPK_0954/0955, TseR/TsiR, the YPK_0952-0958 effector-immunity array); the unrelated OmpR-regulated T6SS-4 cluster and its TssF4; target-cell outer-membrane receptors used for effector uptake (BtuB/OmpF/TolB, OmpC) which belong to prey cells, not the T6SS-3 apparatus. Did NOT mine the RefSeq GenBank; all loci above are taken verbatim from the cited papers. T6SS-3 was confirmed (not T6SS-1/2/4/5) via the effector context YPK_0952 (Song 2020: secretion lost only on clpV3 deletion).
